pr.title: roachprod: propagate errors from managed cluster ASG/LT deletion
pr.body:
Previously, `deleteManagedCluster` silently swallowed errors from
`deleteAutoScalingGroup` and `deleteLaunchTemplate`, logging them as
warnings but always returning nil. This meant that when GC destroyed
a managed cluster, if the ASG deletion succeeded (terminating
instances) but the launch template deletion failed (e.g. due to a
transient AWS API error), the caller believed the cleanup succeeded.
The orphaned launch template then caused subsequent `roachprod create`
calls with the same cluster name to fail with
`InvalidLaunchTemplateName.AlreadyExistsException`.

The fix propagates errors from both ASG and launch template deletion
back to the caller using `errors.CombineErrors`. This ensures that:
1. GC correctly reports failed cluster destructions.
2. The cluster remains discoverable on the next GC cycle for retry,
   since `listManagedLaunchTemplates` creates EmptyCluster placeholder
   VMs for orphaned launch templates.

Resolves: #164987
Epic: none

Release note: None
